items.mistake: Running pip install without activating the env.
items.fix: Activate first (or use the env's python -m pip) and verify with which python.
items.why: Otherwise packages land in system Python and the project still can't find them.
items.example:
```bash
which python      # /usr/bin/python3   ← uh oh
source .venv/bin/activate
which python      # /work/.venv/bin/python  ← good
pip install httpx
```

items.why: The env is large, machine-specific and rebuildable from the manifest.

items.mistake: Expecting venv to install CUDA, ffmpeg or other system libraries.
items.fix: Install system deps via the OS package manager or use conda.
items.why: venv only isolates Python packages, not OS-level binaries.
items.example: `pip install ffmpeg-python` installs the *Python wrapper*; the `ffmpeg` binary still has to come from `apt install ffmpeg` / `brew install ffmpeg` / `conda install ffmpeg`.

items.mistake: Using sudo pip to fix permission errors.
items.fix: Use a virtual environment so installs go somewhere you own.
items.why: sudo pip mutates system Python and can break the OS.
items.example: Permission denied? Don't `sudo pip install pkg` — that writes into `/usr/lib/python3/...`. Activate a venv (or use `--user`); the install lands in a directory you own.
